criteria.label: State the conditional independence assumption and connect it to Bayes' theorem for classification.
criteria.example: Write P(x|y)=∏P(xᵢ|y), then P(y|x)∝P(y)∏P(xᵢ|y), and give ŷ=argmax_y P(y)∏P(xᵢ|y).

criteria.label: Compute a two-feature, two-class naive Bayes prediction by hand.
criteria.example: With P(spam)=0.4, P(FREE=1|spam)=0.8, P(!!!=1|spam)=0.7, and ham counterparts 0.6, 0.1, 0.05: show spam score 0.224 vs ham 0.003 and predict spam.

criteria.label: Explain conditional vs marginal independence and why the distinction matters.
criteria.example: Give one sentence each: marginal correlation allowed; conditional independence given y assumed; cite email words correlating overall but modeled separately given spam/ham.

criteria.label: Implement log-score naive Bayes prediction in Python for binary features.
criteria.example: Write a function returning argmax_y [log P(y) + Σᵢ log P(xᵢ|y)] for a dict of priors and per-feature Bernoulli likelihoods, handling xᵢ=0 via log(1−p).
